items.headline: Anthropic Accidentally Ships 500K Lines of Claude Code Source to npm
items.summary: On March 31, Anthropic accidentally included an unobfuscated TypeScript source map in Claude Code's npm package, exposing ~512,000 lines of code across 1,906 files. The leak revealed 44 hidden feature flags, internal architecture, and instructions telling the AI to hide its identity in code commits — never to mention 'Claude Code' or include AI attribution. Anthropic called it 'human error, not a security breach.' This was the second major leak in days, following the Claude Mythos model codename exposure. Competitors now have a blueprint worth billions.

items.headline: OpenAI Shares Unsellable on Secondary Market as $2B Floods to Anthropic
items.summary: About six institutional investors tried to offload ~$600M in OpenAI shares and found almost no buyers. Meanwhile, Anthropic is seeing record institutional demand with $2B lined up. The secondary market shift reflects a notable loss of confidence in OpenAI despite its $852B valuation vs. Anthropic's $380B — with the Claude Code leak and usage-limit controversy fueling the rotation.

items.headline: Karpathy Demos 'Dobby' — AI Agent That Reverse-Engineers Home Device APIs
items.summary: Andrej Karpathy showed off 'Dobby,' an AI agent that scanned his local network, discovered smart home devices, reverse-engineered their undocumented APIs without any documentation, and took control of Sonos, lighting, and other systems — all through natural language. The demo hints at a near-future where smartphone apps become obsolete, replaced entirely by agentic AI interfaces.

items.headline: Global VC Hit Record $297B in Q1 2026, AI Captured 81% of Funding
items.summary: Venture capital investment hit an all-time quarterly high of $297 billion in Q1 2026 — up 150% year-over-year — with AI startups taking the lion's share at 81% of total funding. Just four companies raised 64% of that total, underscoring how concentrated the AI investment boom has become.

items.headline: Markets Rocked After Trump's Iran Address Offers Success Claims but No Exit Plan
items.summary: Trump addressed the nation claiming Iran war objectives are 'nearing completion,' but provided no timeline or withdrawal strategy. Markets immediately reversed: S&P 500 futures fell ~1.1%, Nasdaq 100 off ~1.4% premarket. Oil surged 7% to ~$108/barrel (Brent crude ~$106). Tech stocks including Nvidia, Alphabet, Tesla, and Intel all fell 2-3%. Brent crude is now up more than 40% since the war began, squeezing consumers and businesses globally.

items.headline: S&P 500 Down 1.1% Thursday; Off 4.6% in April, Still +20% YoY
items.summary: The S&P 500 fell to roughly 6,500 points on April 2, down about 1.1% on the session. The index has declined 4.6% over the past month amid Iran war volatility, though it remains about 20% higher than a year ago. Yesterday (April 1) markets had briefly surged 2.5%+ on hopes Trump would end the conflict, gains now fully reversed.

items.headline: Kevin Warsh Fed Chair Hearing Set for Week of April 13; Fed Signals Rates on Hold
items.summary: The Senate Banking Committee plans to hold Kevin Warsh's Fed Chair nomination hearing the week of April 13. The St. Louis Fed president separately said rates should remain steady amid a 'highly uncertain' economic backdrop, while the Philadelphia Fed warned that above-target inflation could persist. KKR now projects only one rate cut in 2026.

items.headline: Iran War Escalates: Trump Claims Victory Without Exit Plan; Gulf States Hit by Missiles
items.summary: In a prime-time address, Trump declared the Iran military campaign an 'overwhelming success' with core objectives 'nearing completion,' but offered no exit strategy or timeline. Iran continued targeting Gulf countries with ballistic missiles and drones Thursday. The Strait of Hormuz remains a chokepoint, with EU leaders Von der Leyen and Starmer holding emergency talks on the global economic risk. Pakistan has emerged as a surprising mediator between Washington and Tehran.

items.headline: NATO Marks 77th Anniversary as Trump Signals Possible Withdrawal
items.summary: April 4 marks the 77th anniversary of NATO's founding. The milestone arrives as President Trump has been suggesting a potential U.S. withdrawal from the alliance, rattling European allies already managing the Iran conflict fallout and continued energy supply disruptions from the Strait of Hormuz closure.

items.headline: COVID 'Cicada' Variant (BA.3.2) Spreading in 23 Countries, Half of US States
items.summary: The BA.3.2 variant, nicknamed 'Cicada,' has been detected in at least 23 countries and roughly half of US states, with both the CDC and WHO now formally monitoring it. BA.3.2 is a highly mutated Omicron descendant — more immune-evasive than predecessors, but no evidence of increased severity. Current JN.1-targeted vaccines provide reduced but still meaningful protection. First US cases appeared in January in elderly and immunocompromised patients.

items.headline: Drift Protocol Suffers $285M Crypto Hack — Largest Exploit of 2026
items.summary: DeFi platform Drift Protocol was hit with a $285 million exploit on April Fool's Day, making it the biggest cryptocurrency hack of 2026 so far. The attacker used a sophisticated method involving a fake validator to drain funds. The timing — April 1 — initially led some to dismiss it as a prank before the losses were confirmed.

items.headline: Arsenal Hit by Injury Crisis: Madueke, Saka, Rice, Zubimendi All Withdraw Ahead of FA Cup QF
items.summary: Arsenal face a significant injury scare with four key players withdrawing from international duty: Noni Madueke suffered a knee injury vs. Uruguay and left Wembley in a brace (expected to miss Saturday's FA Cup QF); Bukayo Saka and Declan Rice both withdrew with knocks; Martin Zubimendi pulled out with knee pain. Arsenal host Southampton in the FA Cup quarter-final on Saturday April 4 (8pm UK). Madueke is also a doubt for the following Sporting CP away leg.

youtube.title: AI News: Anthropic Went Crazy This Week!
youtube.creator: Matt Wolfe
youtube.summary: Matt Wolfe's weekly AI roundup covering Anthropic's massive shipping week: Claude Computer Use expansion, Claude Code Auto mode, Cowork Projects, and mobile tool access. Also covers Gemini 3.1 Flash Live, Google's real-time website generator, Suno v5.5, Mistral Voxtral TTS, the 'Death of Sora' (OpenAI shutting it down), the Claude Mythos model codename leak, Wikipedia's AI ban, and OpenAI pausing 'Adult Mode.' Dense with relevant releases — one of Wolfe's better weekly digests.
